title: Arlong’s Rule Tightens Around Nami
scene: On pages 6–9, the story moves through Arlong Park and Nami’s dangerous position among the fish-men. The crew follows Nami’s trail toward the island ruled by Arlong and begins to learn how dangerous her home is.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: So, should we eat?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Heck yeah!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: This is exactly what I'm talking about!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: There's something about the place we're going to that you have to know about!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: The reason why the Grand Line is called the "pirates' graveyard,"

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: is because of the 3 great powers that rule over it.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: And one of them,

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Is the Ouka Shichibukai"

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Shichi- Bukai?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Simply put, they're seven pirates that are officially authorized by the world government.

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: What?

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Why would the government permit pirates?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: As long as the Shichibuki give a portion of their loot, obtained from pillaging and uncivilized lands,

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: To the world government, their pirates activities are officially sanctioned.

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Other pirates might look down on them by calling them "government dogs" or whatnot,

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: But they're extremely strong!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: In fact, the Hawk-eyed Mihawk who defeated Brother Zoro,

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Is one of the ouka Shichibukai!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Woah! There's 7 of these guys!?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Those Shichibuki guys are amazing!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: The problem is with another one of the shichibukai.

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: the leader of the fishman pirates, "Jinbe!"

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Fishman? Never met one before!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Fishmen, huh? Isn't fishman Island a very famous travel destination in Grand Line?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: I've heard that the most beautiful mermaids are there.

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: In return for joining the Shichibukai,

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: Jinbe let a terrifying monster loose in East Blue.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Like this?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: That's one ugly fish, haha!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Is it physically impossible for you to pay attention!?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: whatever, I'll skip the complicated history lecture for now.

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: The place we're headed to is "Arlong park"!!!

dialogue.speaker: Arlong
dialogue.text: It's an area ruled by Arlong,

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: A fishman pirate that once fought Arlongside jinbe, a member of the Shichibukai!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: In terms of individual strength, he's far stronger than even don Krieg!

summary: This part builds the Arlong Park conflict by showing how Nami’s choices look contradictory from outside while Arlong’s power keeps the island trapped.
